description:
The sudo <tt>NOPASSWD</tt> tag, when specified, allows a user to execute
commands using sudo without having to authenticate. This should be disabled
by making sure that the <tt>NOPASSWD</tt> tag does not exist in
<tt>/etc/sudoers</tt> configuration file or any sudo configuration snippets
in <tt>/etc/sudoers.d/</tt>.
rationale:
Without re-authentication, users may access resources or perform tasks for which they do not have authorization.

When operating systems provide the capability to escalate a functional capability, it is critical that the user re-authenticate.
ocil_clause: nopasswd is specified in the sudo config files
ocil:
To determine if NOPASSWD has been configured for sudo, run the following command: 
$ sudo grep -ri nopasswd /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.d/
The command should return no output.
fixtext:
Check that Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 is not configured to allow users to execute privileged actions without authenticating.

Remove any occurrence of "NOPASSWD" found in "/etc/sudoers" file or files in the "/etc/sudoers.d" directory.

$ sed -i '/NOPASSWD/ s/^/# /g' /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.d/*
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must require users to provide a password for privilege escalation.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must require users to provide a password for privilege escalation.
stig.vuldiscussion:
Without reauthentication, users may access resources or perform tasks for which they do not have authorization.

When operating systems provide the capability to escalate a functional capability, it is critical that the user reauthenticate.
stig.checktext:
Verify that "/etc/sudoers" has no occurrences of "NOPASSWD" with the following command:

$ sudo grep -ri nopasswd /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.d/

If any occurrences of "NOPASSWD" are returned from the command and have not been documented with the information system security officer (ISSO) as an organizationally defined administrative group using MFA, this is a finding.

title: Ensure Users Re-Authenticate for Privilege Escalation - sudo NOPASSWD
